title: Atelier session artifact
description: The complete persistent state of an Atelier session. Wraps every component referenced elsewhere in the spec: conversation history (with envelopes), cost ledger, recovery log, checkpoint tree, tool-result fixtures, memory, plan, and constraint pins. Replay, fork, and crash recovery all operate on this artifact.

harness_session_version.description: Session-schema version; bumps require a one-way migration tool.

conversation.description: Ordered turns. Each turn carries an envelope per schemas/model_protocol/envelope.v1.json.

tool_name.description: Name of the tool invoked. Required for kind=tool_call so replay can link the ledger entry to the corresponding tool_fixtures entry programmatically (rather than string-parsing the free-form `note`).

freed_tokens.description: Sum of tokens freed across the items replaced by this compaction. Required for kind=compaction.

replaced_items.description: Context-item ids whose contents were summarised and evicted. Required for kind=compaction.

summary_card_id.description: Id of the MemoryCard that took the replaced items' place. Required for kind=compaction. For kind=expansion, the id of the summary card that was just replaced by the restored originals.

expansion_blob_path.description: Repo-relative path to the JSON blob under .atelier/sessions/<sid>/compactions/ that v60.6 Expand will read to restore the originals. Required for kind=compaction.

restored_item_ids.description: Context-item ids that were restored from the blob. Required for kind=expansion.

cache_rewarm_tokens.description: Sum of tokens across the restored items — the cache-rewarm cost the user paid by re-introducing this prefix material into the prompt. Required for kind=expansion.

checkpoints.description: Diff-based per-action checkpoint tree. Forks branch from named checkpoints.

diff_ref.description: Pointer to the stored diff blob

tool_fixtures.description: Recorded input/output per tool call. Replay reads results from here rather than re-executing tools. Each fixture carries either a `result` (successful call) or an `error` (failed call per the §2.5 tool error taxonomy) — never both, never neither.

args.description: v60.37 SCH-M1 — `args` must be a JSON object (matches the wire shape every adapter produces). Catches a misdispatched tool call that smuggled a string or list through earlier loose schema.

error.description: Structured tool failure per spec §2.5 "Tool error model". `kind` matches the ToolError variant; `message` is the rendered Display.

compacted_from.description: Set when this card was generated by §5 non-destructive compaction. Carries the ids of the evicted context items and the relative path to the JSON blob that v60.6 Expand will replay.

cache_rewarm_tokens.description: v60.6 — cache-rewarm cost (sum of `tokens.count` across the items replaced by this card). Set at compaction time so the Expand confirm dialog can surface the cost without reading the blob. Optional for sessions written before v60.6.

constraints.description: Active constraint pins added by the user; reinforced on every subsequent prompt.

recovery_log.description: Partial output from turns interrupted by crash. NOT part of conversation; surfaced as a UI banner so the next turn's model does not misread partial state as completed work.

subagents.description: Sub-agents spawned during this session (§10 delegation mode). Each entry is an isolated §2.5 state-machine run; its conversation rolls up to the parent only via the `spawn_subagent` tool-call result. Keyed by `subagent_id` (matches the `spawn_subagent` tool's output).

parent_turn_id.description: Turn in the parent's `conversation` where `spawn_subagent` was invoked.

subagent_type.description: Name of the registered sub-agent type (matches a `schemas/config/subagent_type.v1.json` manifest).

conversation.description: The sub-agent's full conversation, same shape as the top-level `conversation` field. Persisted for replay/inspection; never injected into the parent's context.

result.description: The single message the sub-agent returned to the parent (the `spawn_subagent` tool-result body).

turn.description: A single conversation turn. Used by both top-level conversation and subagents.*.conversation.

args.description: v60.37 SCH-M1 — `args` must be a JSON object on the conversation turn too.

fixture_ref.description: Pointer into tool_fixtures by tool_call_id; replay reads results from here.
